revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 | 名称 = 七宝瀧寺 | 画像 = [[Image:Shippōryū-ji hondō 2008.jpg|280px]]
本堂 | 所在地 = [[大阪府]][[泉佐野市]]大木8 | 位置 = | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 18.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 14.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 山号 = [[犬鳴山 (大阪府)|犬鳴山]] | 宗派 = [[真言宗犬鳴派]] | 寺格 = [[本山|大本山]] | 本尊 = 倶利伽羅大龍[[不動明王]] | 創建年 = [[斉明天皇]]7年（[[661年]]） | 開基 = [[役小角]] | 正式名 = | 別称 = | 札所等 = [[葛城二十八宿]]修験道根本道場
[[役行者霊蹟札所]]
[[近畿三十六不動尊霊場]]第33番
[[南海沿線七福神]]（[[布袋尊]]）
[[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第54番（大阪第13番） | 文化財 = 絹本著色不動明王二童子四十八使者図、七宝瀧寺 剣 金象嵌銘「国吉」（府指定有形[[文化財]]）
犬鳴山（府の[[名勝]]）
[[日本遺産]]『旅引付と二枚の絵図が伝えるまち』構成文化財
2019年（令和元年）5月20日認定[https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story075/index.html 旅引付と二枚の絵図が伝えるまち-中世日根荘の風景-]（文化庁日本遺産ポータルサイト） | 公式HP = http://www.inunakisan.jp/ | 公式HP名 = 真言宗犬鳴派大本山 犬鳴山七宝瀧寺 | 地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=20|lat_sec=18.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=14.6|label=七宝瀧寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|caption=|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}[[ファイル:Shipporyouji ryoukai no taki1.jpg|thumb|両界の瀧]] '''七宝瀧寺'''（しっぽうりゅうじ）は、[[大阪府]][[泉佐野市]]にある[[真言宗犬鳴派]]の[[本山|大本山]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は犬鳴山（いぬなきさん）。[[本尊]]は倶利伽羅大龍[[不動明王]]。 [[役小角]]が「五百弟子受記品」を埋納したという第八番経塚の所在地・[[犬鳴山 (大阪府)|燈明ヶ岳]]の北側の山中にある。この地域には泉州と紀州を結ぶ南北の交通路と、それと交わる修験の道のり（[[葛城二十八宿]]）があったが、中世の七宝瀧寺はその結び目の一つであったという研究成果もある{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=2}}。 [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）5月20日に認定された[[日本遺産]]『旅引付と二枚の絵図が伝えるまち-中世[[日根荘]]の風景-』の構成文化財のひとつ。「犬鳴山の溪谷」は「[[大阪みどりの百選]]」に選ばれている。 == 歴史 == [[斉明天皇]]7年（[[661年]]）、[[役小角]]によって[[大峰山]]山上ヶ岳が開かれる6年前に開山したと伝わり、元山上と呼ばれている。古くは犬鳴山を含む和泉山系全体を「葛城」と呼び、その中でも犬鳴山は西の行場、東の行場を持つ[[葛城二十八宿]][[修験道]]の根本道場である。 [[平安時代]]初期、大干ばつに見舞われた際に[[雨乞い]]を祈願して雨が降り、それを知った[[淳和天皇]]が「七宝瀧寺」と名付けたという。 [[宇多天皇]]の時代、[[寛平]]2年（[[890年]]）3月、[[紀伊国]]の猟師がこの山域で狩りをしていた際、突然連れていた犬が激しく鳴き出し、結果猟師が射ようとしていた鹿が逃げてしまった。怒った猟師は犬の首をはねたのだが、その首はそれでも飛び跳ね、今まさに猟師に襲いかかろうと狙っていた大蛇に噛み付いた。犬は、主人が大蛇に狙われていることを知って鳴いていたのであった。愛犬に救われたと気付いた猟師は、これを悔いて七宝瀧寺の僧となって愛犬を供養した。 このことを聞いた宇多天皇はいたく感動し、七宝瀧寺に「山号を『いぬなきさん』と改めよ」と勅号を賜った、と伝えられている（義犬伝説）。 [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[正平 (日本)|正平]]17年（[[1362年]]）、南朝方の橋本正高が志一上人（至一上人とも{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019}}）を犬鳴山に招いて不動堂を建立して中興し、さらに塔頭14坊が創建された。当寺は[[室町時代]]には[[熊野信仰]]と葛城修験道の隆盛にしたがって20坊の坊舎を有するようになり、本堂の修復も行われて栄えた。しかし、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]になると[[織田信長]]に寺領を没収され、[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）には[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[紀州征伐|根来攻め]]によって本堂以外の堂舎が焼き払われ、一時は廃絶同然となった。だが、後に秀吉の寄進によって滝本坊が再建され、御供米として30石が寄進されている。 [[江戸時代]]には[[岸和田藩]]主[[岡部行隆]]より新田五反の寄進を受けるなどして、ほぼ今日見られるまでに復興した{{Sfn|大阪の史跡を訪ねて|1974}}。近世初頭の「口上覚」によると、毎年5月に[[高野山]]から葛城巡行する先達たちは、犬鳴山に7日間留まり柴焼護摩を修したといい、「葛城山中で七日間も逗留するのはここだけだ」と述べられており、いかにこの山の地位が高かったかをうかがわせるものとなっている。 [[明治]]時代になると[[廃仏毀釈]]で被害を受けた上に修験道が禁止され、当寺は著しく衰えたが、明治時代後期には再興した。 [[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）8月7日には真言宗犬鳴派を設立し、葛城修験道の根本道場として修験道総本部を設置した。 == 境内 == 参道の両側に神社の瑞垣のような石柱が並んでおり「{{ruby|倶利加羅大龍不動明王|くりからだいりゅうふどうみょうおう}}」「南無神変大菩薩」と染め抜かれた赤や黒ののぼりが立っている。修験道系の寺院であり、多数の祠があり、仏像が並んでいる。石の鳥居の奥には[[布袋尊]]も見えるなど神仏習合色が色濃く見られる。山中には犬鳴川が流れており、そこに「行者の瀧」など計7つの滝と行場がある。 * 本堂 - [[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）再建。本尊の倶利伽羅不動明王像は秘仏であるが、辰年辰の月（現在の4月）、酉年酉の月（現在の9月）に開帳法要が営まれる。 * 鎮守堂 * 清瀧堂 * [[役行者]]像 * 行者の瀧 * 庫裏 * [[布袋]]堂 * 山門 * 鐘楼 * 洗心院 * 心経殿 * 身代り不動明王 * [[空海|弘法大師]]像 * 役行者像 * 志津女の墓 * お志津地蔵尊 * 観音堂 - [[承応]]元年（[[1652年]]）建立。 * 義犬像 * [[虚空蔵菩薩]]殿 * 修験会館（修験道資料館） * 白雲閣 - 宿坊。 * 瑞龍門 * 塔の滝 * 両界の滝 * 岩屋[[大黒天]] === 行場 === 境内を流れる犬鳴川の北側には表行場、南側には裏行場がある。修験道の行場であり、相当に危険な箇所がある。 == 犬鳴渓谷の滝 == 犬鳴渓谷の滝{{cite web |title=犬鳴渓谷の滝 |website=日本の滝（Japan Waterfalls） |url=http://www.japanwaterfalls.com/inunakikeikokunotaki.htm |access-date=2025-01-31}} * 両界の滝 * 塔の滝 * 弁天の滝 * 布引の滝 * 固津喜の滝 * 行者の滝 * 千手の滝 == 文化財 == [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）5月20日、[[文化庁]]により、地域型ストーリーの[[日本遺産]]『旅引付と二枚の絵図が伝えるまち-中世日根荘の風景-』の構成文化財の1つに認定された[http://www.city.izumisano.lg.jp/kakuka/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/menu/nihon_isan/japanheritage.html 令和元年度「日本遺産」に認定されました！]（泉佐野市）。 === 大阪府指定有形文化財 === * 絹本著色不動明王二童子四十八使者図 * 七宝瀧寺 剣 金象嵌銘「国吉」 附：倶利伽羅瀧宝剣拵 === 大阪府指定名勝 === * 犬鳴山 === 大阪市指定有形文化財 === * 絹本著色尊勝曼陀羅図 * 絹本著色[[不動明王]]八大童子図 * 銅鉢 === その他 === * 修験法則 - 天正12年（1592年）宝蔵院秀賢写 * 妙法蓮華経 化城喩品第七残欠 - 平安時代写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=2}} * 箱笈（金銅装水仙・燕子花彫）- 室町時代 * 経筒（陶器製と銅製の二重構造）- 平安時代 * 国々當山修験江掟条々 - 文化11年（1814年）写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=3}} * 葛城峯中之宿次第深秘記 - 室町時代後期写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=4}} * 倶利伽羅龍像 - 江戸時代 * 今度被仰出候本末之儀ニ付口上之覚 - 元禄4年（1691年）写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|pp=6-7}} * 摩利支天法〈正盛追討義親之時云□〉 - 建久6年（1195年）写 * 結縁灌頂雑記 - 永正13年（1516年）俊圓写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=10}} * 吒枳尼天絵像 - 江戸時代後期{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=11}} * 大日経疏縁起 - 室町時代後期写。オリジナルは大永6年（1526年）{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=12}} * 大般若波羅密多経巻五百七 - 治承4年（1180年）写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=13}} == 前後の札所 == ; [[葛城二十八宿]]修験道根本道場 : 第七番経塚 中津川 - '''第八番経塚''' - 第九番経塚 和泉葛城山八大竜王社 ; [[役行者霊蹟札所]] ; [[近畿三十六不動尊霊場]] : 32 [[瀧谷不動明王寺]]　-　'''33 七宝瀧寺'''　-　34 [[根来寺]] ; [[南海沿線七福神]]（布袋尊） ; [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]] : 53 [[水間寺]]　-　'''54 七宝瀧寺'''　-　55 [[金剛寺 (河内長野市)|金剛寺]] == 脚注 == (Temple).svg|marksize=11|caption=|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}[[ファイル:Shipporyouji ryoukai no taki1.jpg|thumb|両界の瀧]] '''七宝瀧寺'''（しっぽうりゅうじ）は、[[大阪府]][[泉佐野市]]にある[[真言宗犬鳴派]]の[[本山|大本山]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は犬鳴山（いぬなきさん）。[[本尊]]は倶利伽羅大龍[[不動明王]]。 [[役小角]]が「五百弟子受記品」を埋納したという第八番経塚の所在地・[[犬鳴山 (大阪府)|燈明ヶ岳]]の北側の山中にある。この地域には泉州と紀州を結ぶ南北の交通路と、それと交わる修験の道のり（[[葛城二十八宿]]）があったが、中世の七宝瀧寺はその結び目の一つであったという研究成果もある{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=2}}。 [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）5月20日に認定された[[日本遺産]]『旅引付と二枚の絵図が伝えるまち-中世[[日根荘]]の風景-』の構成文化財のひとつ。「犬鳴山の溪谷」は「[[大阪みどりの百選]]」に選ばれている。 == 歴史 == [[斉明天皇]]7年（[[661年]]）、[[役小角]]によって[[大峰山]]山上ヶ岳が開かれる6年前に開山したと伝わり、元山上と呼ばれている。古くは犬鳴山を含む和泉山系全体を「葛城」と呼び、その中でも犬鳴山は西の行場、東の行場を持つ[[葛城二十八宿]][[修験道]]の根本道場である。 [[平安時代]]初期、大干ばつに見舞われた際に[[雨乞い]]を祈願して雨が降り、それを知った[[淳和天皇]]が「七宝瀧寺」と名付けたという。 [[宇多天皇]]の時代、[[寛平]]2年（[[890年]]）3月、[[紀伊国]]の猟師がこの山域で狩りをしていた際、突然連れていた犬が激しく鳴き出し、結果猟師が射ようとしていた鹿が逃げてしまった。怒った猟師は犬の首をはねたのだが、その首はそれでも飛び跳ね、今まさに猟師に襲いかかろうと狙っていた大蛇に噛み付いた。犬は、主人が大蛇に狙われていることを知って鳴いていたのであった。愛犬に救われたと気付いた猟師は、これを悔いて七宝瀧寺の僧となって愛犬を供養した。 このことを聞いた宇多天皇はいたく感動し、七宝瀧寺に「山号を『いぬなきさん』と改めよ」と勅号を賜った、と伝えられている（義犬伝説）。 [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[正平 (日本)|正平]]17年（[[1362年]]）、南朝方の橋本正高が志一上人（至一上人とも{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019}}）を犬鳴山に招いて不動堂を建立して中興し、さらに塔頭14坊が創建された。当寺は[[室町時代]]には[[熊野信仰]]と葛城修験道の隆盛にしたがって20坊の坊舎を有するようになり、本堂の修復も行われて栄えた。しかし、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]になると[[織田信長]]に寺領を没収され、[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）には[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[紀州征伐|根来攻め]]によって本堂以外の堂舎が焼き払われ、一時は廃絶同然となった。だが、後に秀吉の寄進によって滝本坊が再建され、御供米として30石が寄進されている。 [[江戸時代]]には[[岸和田藩]]主[[岡部行隆]]より新田五反の寄進を受けるなどして、ほぼ今日見られるまでに復興した{{Sfn|大阪の史跡を訪ねて|1974}}。近世初頭の「口上覚」によると、毎年5月に[[高野山]]から葛城巡行する先達たちは、犬鳴山に7日間留まり柴焼護摩を修したといい、「葛城山中で七日間も逗留するのはここだけだ」と述べられており、いかにこの山の地位が高かったかをうかがわせるものとなっている。 [[明治]]時代になると[[廃仏毀釈]]で被害を受けた上に修験道が禁止され、当寺は著しく衰えたが、明治時代後期には再興した。 [[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）8月7日には真言宗犬鳴派を設立し、葛城修験道の根本道場として修験道総本部を設置した。 == 境内 == 参道の両側に神社の瑞垣のような石柱が並んでおり「{{ruby|倶利加羅大龍不動明王|くりからだいりゅうふどうみょうおう}}」「南無神変大菩薩」と染め抜かれた赤や黒ののぼりが立っている。修験道系の寺院であり、多数の祠があり、仏像が並んでいる。石の鳥居の奥には[[布袋尊]]も見えるなど神仏習合色が色濃く見られる。山中には犬鳴川が流れており、そこに「行者の瀧」など計7つの滝と行場がある。 * 本堂 - [[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）再建。本尊の倶利伽羅不動明王像は秘仏であるが、辰年辰の月（現在の4月）、酉年酉の月（現在の9月）に開帳法要が営まれる。 * 鎮守堂 * 清瀧堂 * [[役行者]]像 * 行者の瀧 * 庫裏 * [[布袋]]堂 * 山門 * 鐘楼 * 洗心院 * 心経殿 * 身代り不動明王 * [[空海|弘法大師]]像 * 役行者像 * 志津女の墓 * お志津地蔵尊 * 観音堂 - [[承応]]元年（[[1652年]]）建立。 * 義犬像 * [[虚空蔵菩薩]]殿 * 修験会館（修験道資料館） * 白雲閣 - 宿坊。 * 瑞龍門 * 塔の滝 * 両界の滝 * 岩屋[[大黒天]] === 行場 === 境内を流れる犬鳴川の北側には表行場、南側には裏行場がある。修験道の行場であり、相当に危険な箇所がある。 == 犬鳴渓谷の滝 == 犬鳴渓谷の滝{{cite web |title=犬鳴渓谷の滝 |website=日本の滝（Japan Waterfalls） |url=http://www.japanwaterfalls.com/inunakikeikokunotaki.htm |access-date=2025-01-31}} * 両界の滝 * 塔の滝 * 弁天の滝 * 布引の滝 * 固津喜の滝 * 行者の滝 * 千手の滝 == 文化財 == [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）5月20日、[[文化庁]]により、地域型ストーリーの[[日本遺産]]『旅引付と二枚の絵図が伝えるまち-中世日根荘の風景-』の構成文化財の1つに認定された[http://www.city.izumisano.lg.jp/kakuka/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/menu/nihon_isan/japanheritage.html 令和元年度「日本遺産」に認定されました！]（泉佐野市）。 === 大阪府指定有形文化財 === * 絹本著色不動明王二童子四十八使者図 * 七宝瀧寺 剣 金象嵌銘「国吉」 附：倶利伽羅瀧宝剣拵 === 大阪府指定名勝 === * 犬鳴山 === 大阪市指定有形文化財 === * 絹本著色尊勝曼陀羅図 * 絹本著色[[不動明王]]八大童子図 * 銅鉢 === その他 === * 修験法則 - 天正12年（1592年）宝蔵院秀賢写 * 妙法蓮華経 化城喩品第七残欠 - 平安時代写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=2}} * 箱笈（金銅装水仙・燕子花彫）- 室町時代 * 経筒（陶器製と銅製の二重構造）- 平安時代 * 国々當山修験江掟条々 - 文化11年（1814年）写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=3}} * 葛城峯中之宿次第深秘記 - 室町時代後期写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=4}} * 倶利伽羅龍像 - 江戸時代 * 今度被仰出候本末之儀ニ付口上之覚 - 元禄4年（1691年）写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|pp=6-7}} * 摩利支天法〈正盛追討義親之時云□〉 - 建久6年（1195年）写 * 結縁灌頂雑記 - 永正13年（1516年）俊圓写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=10}} * 吒枳尼天絵像 - 江戸時代後期{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=11}} * 大日経疏縁起 - 室町時代後期写。オリジナルは大永6年（1526年）{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=12}} * 大般若波羅密多経巻五百七 - 治承4年（1180年）写{{Sfn|和歌山大学|2019|p=13}} == 前後の札所 == ; [[葛城二十八宿]]修験道根本道場 : 第七番経塚 中津川 - '''第八番経塚''' - 第九番経塚 和泉葛城山八大竜王社 ; [[役行者霊蹟札所]] ; [[近畿三十六不動尊霊場]] : 32 [[瀧谷不動明王寺]]　-　'''33 七宝瀧寺'''　-　34 [[根来寺]] ; [[南海沿線七福神]]（布袋尊） ; [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]] : 53 [[水間寺]]　-　'''54 七宝瀧寺'''　-　55 [[金剛寺 (河内長野市)|金剛寺]] == 脚注 == Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōki 8, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|20|18.6|N|135|23|14.6|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shippōryū-ji}} | Mountain name = {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Inunaki Sect | Rank = Grand Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = 661 CE (Empress Saimei's reign) | Founder = {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Cultural properties = Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō and attendants; Kurikara sword with gold inlay inscription "Kuniyoshi"; Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property
Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft}} founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of {{ill|Katsuragi 28 Lodges|ja|葛城二十八宿|1=Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Fudō Myōō temples]] [[Category:Izumisano]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[Category:Chintaku Reifu]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] }} [[en:draft:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[sn:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[ja:七宝瀧寺]] [[sn:hipporyuji Temple]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shipporyuji Temple}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft}} founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of {{ill|Katsuragi 28 Lodges|ja|葛城二十八宿|1=Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shipporyuji Temple}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Fudō Myōō temples]] [[Category:Izumisano]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[Category:Chintaku Reifu]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] }} [[every:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[en:draft:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[sn:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[ja:七宝瀧寺]] [[sn:hipporyuji Temple]]

In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shipporyuji Temple}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Fudō Myōō temples]] [[Category:Izumisano]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[Category:Chintaku Reifu]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Shipporyuji Temple]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[en:draft:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[sn:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[ja:七宝瀧寺]] [[sn:hipporyuji Temple]]

Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft}} founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of {{ill|Katsuragi 28 Lodges|ja|葛城二十八宿|1=Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Fudō Myōō temples]] [[Category:Izumisano]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[Category:Chintaku Reifu]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Shipporyuji Temple]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[en:draft:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[sn:Shipporyuji Temple]] [[ja:七宝瀧寺]] [[sn:hipporyuji Temple]]

Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumisano|qq=}}, Ōki 8, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|20|18.6|N|135|23|14.6|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shippōryū-ji}} | Mountain name = {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} | Sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} Inunaki Sect | Rank = Grand Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} | Founded = 661 CE (Empress Saimei's reign) | Founder = {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}} | Cultural properties = Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō and attendants; Kurikara sword with gold inlay inscription "Kuniyoshi"; Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property
Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Inunaki Sect|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumisano|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendō|qq=}} practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}} founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of {{ill|Katsuragi 28 Lodges|ja|葛城二十八宿|1=Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi 28 Lodges|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qq=}}, a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Station|qq=}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Shrine|qq=}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryuji|qq=}} The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Station|qq=}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Shrine|qq=}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shipporyuji Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shipporyuji Temple|Shipporyuji Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japan temple infobox | Name = Shippōryū-ji (七宝瀧寺) | image = [[Image:Shippōryū-ji hondō 2008.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumisano|qq=}}, Ōki 8, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|20|18.6|N|135|23|14.6|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shippōryū-ji}} | Mountain name = {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} | Sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} Inunaki Sect | Rank = Grand Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} | Founded = 661 CE (Empress Saimei's reign) | Founder = {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}} | Cultural properties = Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō and attendants; Kurikara sword with gold inlay inscription "Kuniyoshi"; Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property
Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Inunaki Sect|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Izumisano|ja|泉佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumisano|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugendō|qq=}} practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}} founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of {{ill|Katsuragi 28 Lodges|ja|葛城二十八宿|1=Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi 28 Lodges|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi 28 Shuku|qq=}}, a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from {{ill|Hineno Station|ja|日根野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Station|qq=}} (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Shrine|qq=}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryuji|qq=}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbo Kojin|qq=}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shipporyuji Temple|qq=}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Mandaraji|lt=Mandaraji|lt=Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|qq=}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanno Gongen|qq=}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|qq=}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japan temple infobox | Name = Shippōryū-ji (七宝瀧寺) | image = [[Image:Shippōryū-ji hondō 2008.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Ōki 8, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|20|18.6|N|135|23|14.6|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shippōryū-ji}} | Mountain name = {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} | Sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] Inunaki Sect | Rank = Grand Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} | Founded = 661 CE (Empress Saimei's reign) | Founder = [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] | Cultural properties = Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō and attendants; Kurikara sword with gold inlay inscription "Kuniyoshi"; Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property
Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Inunaki Sect|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of [[:en:Katsuragi 28 Shuku|Katsuragi 28 Shuku]], a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from [[:en:Hineno Station|Hineno Station]] (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Shrine|qq=}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryuji|qq=}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbo Kojin|qq=}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shipporyuji Temple|qq=}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Mandaraji|lt=Mandaraji|lt=Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|qq=}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|lt=Sannō Gongen|lt=Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanno Gongen|qq=}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|qq=}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Fudō Myōō temples]] inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shipporyuji Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shipporyuji Temple|Shipporyuji Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japan temple infobox | Name = Shippōryū-ji (七宝瀧寺) | image = [[Image:Shippōryū-ji hondō 2008.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Ōki 8, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|20|18.6|N|135|23|14.6|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shippōryū-ji}} | Mountain name = {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} | Sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] Inunaki Sect | Rank = Grand Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} | Founded = 661 CE (Empress Saimei's reign) | Founder = [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] | Cultural properties = Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō and attendants; Kurikara sword with gold inlay inscription "Kuniyoshi"; Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property
Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Inunaki Sect|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of [[:en:Katsuragi 28 Shuku|Katsuragi 28 Shuku]], a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from [[:en:Hineno Station|Hineno Station]] (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Shrine|qq=}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryuji|qq=}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbo Kojin|qq=}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shipporyuji Temple|qq=}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Mandaraji|lt=Mandaraji|lt=Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|qq=}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|lt=Sannō Gongen|lt=Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanno Gongen|qq=}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|qq=}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Fudō Myōō temples]] Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Ōki 8, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|20|18.6|N|135|23|14.6|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shippōryū-ji}} | Mountain name = {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} | Sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] Inunaki Sect | Rank = Grand Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} | Founded = 661 CE (Empress Saimei's reign) | Founder = [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] | Cultural properties = Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō and attendants; Kurikara sword with gold inlay inscription "Kuniyoshi"; Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property
Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Inunaki Sect|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of [[:en:Katsuragi 28 Shuku|Katsuragi 28 Shuku]], a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from [[:en:Hineno Station|Hineno Station]] (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Shrine|qq=}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryuji|qq=}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbo Kojin|qq=}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shipporyuji Temple|qq=}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Mandaraji|lt=Mandaraji|lt=Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|qq=}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|lt=Sannō Gongen|lt=Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanno Gongen|qq=}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|qq=}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shipporyuji Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shipporyuji Temple|Shipporyuji Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japan temple infobox | Name = Shippōryū-ji (七宝瀧寺) | image = [[Image:Shippōryū-ji hondō 2008.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall | Location = [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Ōki 8, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|20|18.6|N|135|23|14.6|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shippōryū-ji}} | Mountain name = {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} | Sect = [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] Inunaki Sect | Rank = Grand Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}} | Founded = 661 CE (Empress Saimei's reign) | Founder = [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] | Cultural properties = Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō and attendants; Kurikara sword with gold inlay inscription "Kuniyoshi"; Osaka Prefectural Cultural Property
Inunakisan designated as an Osaka Prefectural Scenic Site
Constituent cultural property of the 2019 Japanese Heritage "Landscapes of Medieval Hineno-shō" | Website = [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Site of Shippōryū-ji] }} '''Shippōryū-ji''' (七宝瀧寺) is a Grand Head Temple of the {{ill|Shingon Inunaki Sect|ja|真言宗犬鳴派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Inunaki Sect|qq=}}, located in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The temple's mountain name is {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|ja|倶利伽羅大龍不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurikara Dai Ryū Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}. Shippōryū-ji has been recognized as a central location for [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] practices and holds historical and spiritual significance. == History == The temple is said to have been established in 661 CE, six years before [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]] founded Mount Ōmine. Historically, it served as a pivotal hub connecting the routes of [[:en:Katsuragi 28 Shuku|Katsuragi 28 Shuku]], a Shugendō path. In the medieval period, Shippōryū-ji was an integral part of the cultural and spiritual landscape linking Izumi and Kii Provinces. Shippōryū-ji's history includes associations with legends such as the "Dog Crying Legend" (義犬伝説), which inspired the mountain's name, {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}}. During the Heian period, Emperor Junna named the temple "Shippōryū-ji" following a successful rain prayer. Despite destruction during the Sengoku period, it was rebuilt with support from Toyotomi Hideyoshi and others. By the Edo period, Shippōryū-ji became a prominent Shugendō training site and received additional land donations. The temple continued to thrive until the Meiji Restoration's anti-Buddhist movement and Shugendō prohibition, after which it was revitalized in the late Meiji era. In 1950, Shippōryū-ji established the Inunaki Sect of Shingon Buddhism and resumed its role as a spiritual center for Shugendō. == Buildings and Grounds == The temple complex features both historical and Shugendō-related structures, including: * '''Main Hall''': Houses the hidden image of Kurikara Fudō Myōō, revealed in specific years during special ceremonies. * '''Seven Waterfalls''': Includes training sites like "Ryōkai Falls." * '''Shugendō Training Grounds''': North and south of the Inunaki River. * '''Cultural Properties''': Structures such as the Hondō (Main Hall), Kannon-dō (Kannon Hall), and numerous stone carvings and statues. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Hanging scroll of Fudō Myōō with attendants * Kurikara Sword with gold inlay inscription === Designated Scenic Site === * {{ill|Inunakisan|ja|犬鳴山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inunakisan|qq=}} === Other Cultural Treasures === * Manuscripts and artifacts related to Shugendō practices * Historical documents from the Muromachi and Edo periods == Access == ; By Train * Approximately 10 km from [[:en:Hineno Station|Hineno Station]] (JR Hanwa Line) ; By Road * Accessible via Osaka Prefectural Route 62 and other local roads. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hineno Shrine|ja|日根野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hineno Shrine|qq=}} * Other Shugendō pilgrimage spots in the region. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.inunakisan.jp/ Official Website of Shippōryū-ji] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/inunakisanshipporyuji Osaka Info - Shippōryū-ji] ==Gallery== File:Asashiro-jinja03s3200.jpg|The main hall and Inari shrine (Inari [[Myōjin|Omyojin]]/[[Dakini]]) of {{ill|Enryu-ji|lt=Enryu-ji|lt=Enryuji|ja|円隆寺 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryuji|qq=}} File:七宝瀧寺鎮守堂_泉佐野市_2013.11.23_-_panoramio.jpg|{{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}}・{{ill|Sanbo Kojin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbo Kojin|qq=}}（{{ill|Shipporyuji Temple|ja|七宝瀧寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shipporyuji Temple|qq=}}） File:観心寺にて_鎮守堂(訶梨帝母天堂)_2013.3.15_-_panoramio.jpg|[[Hariti]] Hall（[[Kanshin-ji]]） File:Gahaishizan_Mandaraji_05.JPG|Chinju-dō、[[Dō (architecture)|Dō]]、[[Homa (ritual)|Homa]]（{{ill|Mandaraji|lt=Mandaraji|lt=Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|ja|曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gahaishizan Mandaraji Temple|qq=}}） File:Makisan_Chofukuji_07.JPG|{{ill|Sannō Gongen|lt=Sannō Gongen|lt=Sanno Gongen|ja|山王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanno Gongen|qq=}} Hall ({{ill|Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|lt=Makiyama Hannyuin Chou Temple|ja|長福寺 (美作市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choufukuji Temple (Mimasaka City)|qq=}}) ==See Also== {{ill|Chintaku Reifu|ja|鎮宅霊符神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|七宝瀧寺|version=104668674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Fudō Myōō temples]]
